jobs: DSCR Lead Pipeline Daily (6b250f66, Mon-Fri only)
collisions.reason: 3 heavy lead-generation jobs fire at exactly 15:00 ET. All require LLM orchestration, external API calls (BatchData skip trace, Parse.bot scraping, ActiveCampaign uploads), and significant execution time (56-88 seconds each). Exceeds the 4-session concurrency limit when combined with hourly jobs.

4 hourly/30-min trading jobs overlap with the 15:00 lead-gen burst. Combined with the 3 lead-gen jobs, this creates a 7-job pileup against a 4-session concurrency limit. This is the primary cause of the rate_limit cascades observed in failure data.

jobs: Memecoin Scanner (bac2e969, 8x daily at odd hours incl 21:00)
collisions.reason: 3 jobs at 21:00 ET. Evening Revenue Report and Daily Lead CSV both use LLM + email sending. Memecoin Scanner adds LLM load. Moderate contention but all are lighter jobs (18-75 second durations).
2 jobs staggered by 5 minutes. Both are lightweight reporting jobs. Low contention.

Single job but uses claude-sonnet (heavier model) with 900s timeout and includes image analysis for photo screening. Duration averages 520 seconds — long-running job that blocks a session slot. Overlaps with hourly trading scanners.

4 hourly jobs all using LLM tokens. Combined with the 30-min Situation Monitor, this means 5 trading-related jobs compete for the same resources every hour. Each uses 20-70 seconds of LLM time. When they align (same anchor), all 4 fire within seconds of each other.
Runs every 30 minutes, doubling the frequency of hourly jobs. Creates additional contention points at the half-hour mark when combined with the 4 hourly scanners.
4 bi-hourly jobs. Quant Research uses claude-sonnet with 900s timeout (heavy). Pattern Detection and Total Recall jobs are lighter. When these align with hourly scanners, session contention spikes.
Single job with built-in 5-minute stagger offset. Duration is short (11 seconds). Low contention.
Runs during low-activity hours. Only hourly scanners overlap. Duration ~60 seconds.
Fires 1 hour before the 15:00 lead-gen burst. If it runs long, it holds a session slot into the peak contention window.

peak_contention_analysis.observed_failure_pattern: Rate limit cascades at 15:00 ET are the most common failure mode. The 4-session concurrency limit means 3 jobs queue or fail. LLM token consumption from 7 simultaneous jobs triggers Anthropic rate limiting, causing 'Provider anthropic is in cooldown' errors that cascade to ALL jobs for 5-15 minutes.

recommendations: Stagger the 15:00 ET lead-gen jobs: move DSCR Lead Pipeline to 15:30, Parse.bot Daily Scraper to 14:30 — eliminates the 3-job simultaneous burst
Stagger hourly trading scanners: Glint at :00, Kalshi at :15, Paper Trading at :30, Polymarket at :45 — prevents 4-job LLM pileup
Reduce Situation Monitor from 30min to 1h interval — current 30min frequency doubles contention and historically had the most timeout failures (200+ timeouts)
Increase maxConcurrentSessions from 4 to 6 — the system regularly attempts 5-7 concurrent jobs during peak hours
Move the Daily Cash-Flowing Deals Email (12:00, 520s avg duration, uses Sonnet) to 11:00 or 13:00 to avoid overlapping with hourly scanner alignment
Add stagger offsets (staggerMs) to all hourly jobs — currently only MiroFish and Pattern Detection have stagger configured
Move Tax Deed Property Scanner from 14:00 to 13:00 to create a buffer before the 15:00 peak
Consider splitting the 21:00 cluster: move Daily Lead CSV to 20:30 or Evening Revenue Report to 21:30
